title: Parts of Speech
summary: The 8 building blocks of English: noun, pronoun, verb, adjective, adverb, preposition, conjunction, interjection.

title: Nouns
summary: People, places, things and ideas — common/proper, countable/uncountable, singular/plural.

title: Pronouns
summary: Words that replace nouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they, and possessives.

title: Articles: a, an, the
summary: When to use a, an, the — and when to use no article at all.

title: Adjectives
summary: Words that describe nouns, and the order adjectives go in.

title: Verbs
summary: Action and state words — the heart of every sentence.

title: Adverbs
summary: Words that describe verbs, adjectives and other adverbs (often -ly).

title: Prepositions
summary: Small words of place, time and direction: in, on, at, to, from.

title: Conjunctions
summary: Joining words: and, but, or, because, so, although.

title: Sentence Structure (SVO)
summary: Subject + Verb + Object — how a basic English sentence is built.

title: Subject–Verb Agreement
summary: Making the verb match the subject (he goes, they go).

title: Present Simple
summary: Habits, facts and routines: I work, she works.

title: Present Continuous
summary: Actions happening now: I am working.

title: Past Simple
summary: Finished actions in the past: I worked, I went.

title: Past Continuous
summary: Actions in progress in the past: I was working.

title: Future: will and going to
summary: Two ways to talk about the future and when to use each.

title: Asking Questions
summary: Yes/No questions and Wh- questions (what, where, when, why, how).

title: Negatives
summary: Making sentences negative with not, do not, did not.

title: There is / There are
summary: Saying that something exists.

title: Punctuation Basics
summary: Full stops, commas, question marks and capital letters.

title: Present Perfect
summary: Past actions with a link to now: I have finished.

title: Present Perfect Continuous
summary: Ongoing actions up to now: I have been working.

title: Past Perfect
summary: The earlier of two past actions: I had left before she came.

title: Past Perfect Continuous
summary: Duration before a past moment: I had been waiting.

title: Future Continuous & Perfect
summary: I will be working; I will have finished.

title: Modal Verbs
summary: can, could, may, might, must, should, have to — ability, advice, obligation.

title: Zero & First Conditional
summary: Real situations: If you heat ice, it melts; If it rains, I will stay.

title: Second & Third Conditional
summary: Unreal/hypothetical: If I were rich…; If I had known…

title: Passive Voice
summary: When the action matters more than who did it: The cake was eaten.

title: Reported Speech
summary: Telling what someone said: She said she was tired.

title: Gerunds & Infinitives
summary: -ing vs to + verb: I enjoy reading / I want to read.

title: Comparatives & Superlatives
summary: bigger, the biggest; more useful, the most useful.

title: Relative Clauses
summary: Adding detail with who, which, that, where.

title: Quantifiers
summary: some, any, much, many, a few, a little, a lot of.

title: Phrasal Verbs
summary: Verb + particle meanings: give up, look after, run out of.

title: used to / would
summary: Talking about past habits and states.

title: Question Tags
summary: …isn’t it? …don’t you? — checking and confirming.

title: Mixed Conditionals
summary: Mixing time frames: If I had studied, I would be a doctor now.

title: The Subjunctive
summary: I suggest that he be present; If I were you…

title: Inversion
summary: Negative fronting for emphasis: Never have I seen…

title: Cleft Sentences
summary: Emphasis with It is… and What… : It was John who called.

title: Participle Clauses
summary: Tightening sentences: Walking home, I saw…

title: Causative (have/get something done)
summary: I had my car repaired.

title: Advanced Reported Speech
summary: Reporting verbs, tense shifts and nuance.

title: Articles: Advanced Usage
summary: Subtle a/an/the/zero choices that trip up learners.

title: Connectors & Cohesion
summary: however, therefore, nevertheless, in spite of — linking ideas smoothly.

title: Advanced Punctuation
summary: Semicolons, colons, dashes and the Oxford comma.

title: Ellipsis & Substitution
summary: Leaving words out naturally: I can if you can.

title: Fronting & Emphasis
summary: Reordering sentences for stylistic effect.

title: Hedging & Nuance
summary: Softening claims: it tends to, arguably, somewhat.

title: Discourse Markers
summary: actually, mind you, to be fair — sounding natural.

title: Formal vs Informal Register
summary: Choosing the right tone for the situation.

title: Common Advanced Errors
summary: Subtle mistakes even strong learners make.
